Name: Andvari, the Pike
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Andvaranaut, the Cursed Ring' from your deck.
Anecdote: 'The dwarf swam in the waterfall in the shape of a pike. Loki netted him and took all his gold — and the last ring from his hand. So Andvari cursed it: it shall be the death of whoever owns it.'

Name: Andvaranaut, the Cursed Ring
Effect: On enter: You may gift this card to an opponent. At the end of each turn, if this is on top of a stack, destroy the being beneath it.
Anecdote: 'The ring bred gold as long as its curse slept. It never slept long.'

Name: Barnstokkr, the Great Oak
Effect: On enter: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may draw an artefact from among them; put the rest at the bottom.
Anecdote: 'A one-eyed stranger drove a sword to the hilt into the tree-trunk: For him who can draw it. Every man pulled; it came to Sigmund's hand as if it lay loose.'

Name: Regin, the Smith
Effect: On enter: Draw 'Gram, the Broken Sword' from your deck or underworld.
Anecdote: 'He whetted the shards and forged them anew, and fire seemed to burn along the edges. Now kill the dragon, said Regin — my brother.'

Name: Grani, Sigurd's Horse
Effect: While on top: Once per turn, you may move Grani and the card beneath it to another location.
Anecdote: 'The grey colt would let no other man ride him. He was of Sleipnir's line, and an old one-eyed man had helped choose him.'

Name: Gudrun, Daughter of Grimhild
Effect: On enter: Return an enemy being with a cost of [2] or less to its owner's hand.
Anecdote: 'Her mother brewed the ale of forgetting, and Sigurd drank it, and Brynhild's name went out of him like a fire quenched.'

Name: Sinfjotli, the Wolf-Skinned
Anecdote: 'Father and son put on the wolf-shirts and could not take them off. Nine days they ran the forest as wolves, and what they met, they killed.'

Name: Sigmund, Wielder of Gram
Anecdote: 'In his last battle a one-eyed man in a broad hat raised his spear, and the sword broke upon it. Keep the shards, said Sigmund, dying. My son will bear them.'

Name: Fafnir's Hoard
Effect: On enter: Get an extra mana crystal for each monster in your underworld.
Anecdote: 'Load the gold on Grani's back. There was more of it than two horses could bear, and the curse rode with every ring of it.'

Name: Gram, the Broken Sword
Effect: Costs [2] less if 'Regin, the Smith' is in play.
Anecdote: 'Sigurd struck the anvil, and the sword split it to the base. Then he carried it to the river, and it cut a tuft of wool drifting on the current.'

Name: Sigurd, Dragon-Slayer
Effect: When Sigurd defeats a monster, draw two cards.
Anecdote: 'The dragon's blood touched his tongue, and the speech of birds came to him. The birds said: Regin means to betray you.'

Name: Brynhild, the Valkyrie
Effect: On destruction: Destroy your strongest hero at this location.
Anecdote: 'She swore to wed only the man who knew no fear, and ringed her hall with fire. Two men rode through it; one wore the other's face.'

Name: Gunnar, the Harper
Effect: On destruction: Each opponent must banish their weakest being.
Anecdote: 'Cast bound into the snake-pit, he played the harp with his toes so sweetly that every serpent slept — save one.'

Name: Volsung, the Old King
Anecdote: 'I spoke a vow unborn: I shall flee neither fire nor iron for fear. So I have done all my life — and he fell with his men, keeping it.'

Name: Fafnir, the Hoard-Dragon
Effect: Cannot be played at a location where you have heroes. Defeated when you have one or more heroes here. Reward: Draw three cards, and you may play 'Andvaranaut, the Cursed Ring' from your deck for free.
Anecdote: 'He was a dwarf's son once. He lay so long on the gold, and grudged it so bitterly, that he became the worm he was.'
